tool_design.role_in_intervention: Structured measurement layer for whether communication culture is actually improving.
tool_design.expected_output: Scored survey results with action priorities

failure_warnings.warning: Scoring or survey interpretation is inconsistent.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Trend comparisons become weak and trust falls.
early_signals: Different reviewers read the same result differently

failure_warnings.warning: Survey or audit findings do not turn into visible action.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The team sees measurement without change.
early_signals: Action register stays vague or empty

content:
# Communication Culture Survey & Scorecard **Tool ID:** T076-05 **Block:** B076 (Communication Culture & Feedback) **Domain:** Team & Talent (D04_team_talent) **Level:** L3 **Format:** Audit **Role:** Owner / Manager **Time Required:** 20 minutes to run, 20 minutes to review, 15 minutes to action **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For This is the periodic check on whether the communication culture is actually improving or just being talked about. Use it to measure: - psychological safety - trust in management communication - quality of feedback - clarity of handovers and updates - whether concerns are raised early Run it quarterly in fast-moving teams or at least annually in stable teams. --- ## Survey Instructions Ask the team to score each statement from 1 to 5. **Scale** - 1 = strongly disagree - 2 = disagree - 3 = mixed / unsure - 4 = agree - 5 = strongly agree Keep it anonymous where possible. --- ## Survey Questions | # | Statement | Score 1-5 | |---|---|---| | 1 | I feel safe to raise a concern here. | | | 2 | If I make a mistake, it is handled fairly. | | | 3 | Important information usually reaches me in time. | | | 4 | Daily briefings and handovers help me do my job well. | | | 5 | Feedback here is usually specific and useful. | | | 6 | Managers deal with issues early rather than letting them build. | | | 7 | I can disagree respectfully without fear of backlash. | | | 8 | Team conflict is addressed, not ignored. | | | 9 | Good communication is recognised here, not just mistakes. | | | 10 | I trust management to respond if I raise something important. | | **Optional open questions** - What helps communication here? - What gets in the way? - What should we change in the next 30 days? --- ## Scorecard | Area | Questions | Average Score | Status | |---|---|---|---| | Psychological safety | 1, 2, 7 | | Red / Amber / Green | | Information flow | 3, 4 | | Red / Amber / Green | | Feedback quality | 5, 9 | | Red / Amber / Green | | Management trust | 6, 10 | | Red / Amber / Green | | Conflict handling | 8 | | Red / Amber / Green | **Overall average:** ____________________ **Interpretation** - **4.2-5.0** = strong and stable - **3.5-4.1** = functional but fragile - **2.8-3.4** = inconsistent and risky - **below 2.8** = urgent attention needed --- ## Action Register | Finding | Action | Owner | Due Date | Status | |---|---|---|---|---| | | | | | Open / In progress / Closed | | | | | | Open / In progress / Closed | | | | | | Open / In progress / Closed | --- ## What To Do With The Results
  • Share the headline results with the team.
  • Pick no more than three actions for the next cycle.
  • Assign owners and dates immediately.
  • Re-test the weakest area next quarter.
Do not run a survey if you are not prepared to respond visibly. --- ## Common Mistakes - Running the survey too often and taking no action. - Making it anonymous in theory but not in practice. - Hiding weak scores from the team. - Collecting comments but never grouping themes. - Trying to solve five weak areas at once. --- ## Connected Resources **Used alongside:** - T076-02 - Communication Charter Workshop Template - T076-03 - Conflict Resolution Pathway & Case Log - T076-04 - Difficult Conversations Guide **Parent block:** B076 - Communication Culture & Feedback --- *Tool Version 2.0 - built to turn soft culture talk into visible signals and actions.*
